engagement_title: Patient Readmission Risk Scoring for Regional Health System

company_profile.business_model: Regional health system operating 4 hospitals and 12 outpatient clinics across two states

problem_statement: The health system had a 30-day readmission rate of 18.4%, above the national average of 15.5%. Care coordinators manually reviewed discharge summaries to flag high-risk patients but covered only 30% of daily discharges due to staffing constraints. CMS penalties for excess readmissions had cost the system $1.2M in the prior fiscal year.
solution_summary: Tenex built a readmission risk scoring model using 3 years of EHR data — diagnosis codes, prior admissions, medication adherence flags, and social determinants of health. A gradient boosting classifier scored each discharge within 2 hours of discharge orders being placed. Scores above 0.65 triggered an automatic care coordinator assignment in the Epic workflow. The model was deployed as a REST service integrated with Epic FHIR API.

primary_metric.label: 30-day Readmission Rate
primary_metric.outcome: 14.1% readmission rate — 120 fewer readmissions annually, $1.8M CMS penalty avoidance

secondary_metrics.label: Model precision at 0.65 threshold
secondary_metrics.value: 71% — 7 in 10 flagged patients readmitted within 30 days

secondary_metrics.label: Time to score
secondary_metrics.value: Under 2 hours from discharge order placement

results.measurement_basis: Compared to same 6-month window in prior year, adjusted for case mix index

engagement_details.client_team_involvement: Chief Medical Informatics Officer, 2 Epic analysts, 3 care coordinators as SMEs during model validation

tech_stack.ml_approach: Gradient boosting classifier (XGBoost) with SHAP explainability layer for care coordinator transparency
infrastructure: Cloud Run for FHIR API service

lessons_learned: HIPAA BAA setup added 3 weeks to the engagement start. Build legal lead time into healthcare scoping. SHAP explainability was essential — care coordinators rejected the model without it.
embed_text:
Healthcare — Mid-market regional health system, 1200 employees, ~$200M revenue, US Southeast

Problem: 30-day readmission rate of 18.4% above 15.5% national average. Care coordinators manually reviewed only 30% of daily discharges. CMS penalties cost $1.2M annually.

Solution: Tenex built a readmission risk scoring model using 3 years of EHR data with XGBoost classifier. Scores each discharge within 2 hours. Threshold 0.65 triggers automatic care coordinator assignment in Epic workflow.

Approach: XGBoost gradient boosting with SHAP explainability, Epic FHIR API integration

Results: 30-day readmission rate: 18.4% to 14.1% (120 fewer readmissions, $1.8M CMS penalty avoidance) (6 months post-deployment). Care coordinator coverage: 30% to 89% of high-risk discharges.
industry_benchmark: National average 30-day readmission rate 15.5%; CMS penalty threshold at 17.5% for comparable DRG mix
success_threshold: Requires Epic or equivalent EHR with FHIR API access and at least 2 years of structured discharge history. Care coordinator team must have capacity to act on scored alerts within 4 hours.
gap_analysis_template: Maturity gap of {gap} points from engagement baseline — readmission risk ROI realises within 6 months when EHR data is centralised and care coordinator workflow can receive automated assignments.
